name: CFOP Simplifié (2-Look LL)
description: The most popular intermediate step after the beginner method. Uses the same CFOP framework (Cross, F2L) but solves the last layer with a 2-look approach: OCLL to orient corners, then EPLL to permute edges. Edge orientation (EOLL, 3 trivial cases) and corner permutation (CPLL, 2 cases) are done beforehand with simple algorithms, so the real recognition work is only 2 looks. Requires about 16 algorithms total and is a natural stepping stone toward full CFOP.

steps.name: Cross
steps.description: Solve the 4 edge pieces of one face on the bottom layer, ensuring each edge matches its adjacent center color. Same as full CFOP cross. Plan during inspection.
tips: Always solve the cross on the bottom

steps.name: F2L (First Two Layers)
steps.description: Solve the first two layers by pairing corners with edges and inserting them into slots. At this stage, use intuitive F2L — understand how pieces move rather than memorizing algorithms.
tips: Learn intuitive F2L first, not algorithms
Use wide moves (Rw, Lw) to reduce rotations
Do not rush — understanding beats speed

steps.name: OLL (2-Look: EOLL → OCLL)
steps.description: Orient all last layer pieces in 2 steps. First, make a yellow cross with EOLL (3 trivial cases: dot → line → cross, using F R U R' U' F' and its variant). Then orient all corners with OCLL (7 cases: sune, anti-sune, H, pi, headlights, bowtie, chameleon). EOLL is almost automatic; OCLL is the real recognition step.
tips: EOLL is only 3 patterns — learn it in 10 minutes
OCLL is the first real algorithm set to master
Sune and Anti-Sune cover most OCLL cases
Hold the pattern in the correct orientation before executing

steps.name: PLL (2-Look: CPLL → EPLL)
steps.description: Permute all last layer pieces in 2 steps. First, swap corners into position with CPLL (2 cases: adjacent swap = T-perm, diagonal swap = Y-perm). Then permute edges with EPLL (4 cases: Ua, Ub, H, Z). CPLL is just 'headlights or no headlights'; EPLL is the real recognition step.
tips: CPLL: headlights on one side = adjacent swap, no headlights = diagonal swap
EPLL: find the solved edge and hold it at the back
M-slice moves make EPLL very fast
Learn Ua and Ub first — they cover most EPLL cases

pros: Only 16 algorithms total vs 78 for full CFOP
2-look recognition: only OCLL and EPLL require real pattern matching
Natural progression — add full OLL and PLL later
Achievable sub-45 to sub-30 with practice
Uses the same F2L as full CFOP
cons: Last layer takes 2-4 seconds longer than full CFOP
4 algorithm steps but only 2 real recognition steps
Some algorithms overlap with full OLL/PLL but are not the same
creator: Jessica Fridrich
